questions.question:
Read the following passage from paragraph 10:

“I closed my eyes. I saw Dad standing on a stage, his head bowed, his name written in large black letters, and then crossed out in red ink, on a sign hanging from his neck. I saw myself standing in the middle of the stage, facing thousands of people, condemning Dad for his crimes, raising my fist to lead the chant, ‘Down with Jiang Xi-reng.’ I saw Dad looking at me hopelessly, tears on his face.”

What does this suggest about Ji-li's relationship with her father?

questions.question: Which of the following best expresses a central idea of the excerpt?

responses.content: C. If someone does not do what the Communists tell them, they are likely to face a harsh punishment.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text most strongly supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: B. “You can break with your family and follow Chairman Mao, or you can follow your father and become an enemy of the people.”

questions.question: How does the setting in paragraphs 12-14 cause Brian to change from feeling hopeless to taking action?

responses.content: Brian evaluates his surroundings  so carefully because he is trying to find a source of food

questions.question: This part of the story takes place in the wilderness. What challenge does this setting create for Brian?

responses.content: C. The setting's lack of food sources forces Brian to confront the reality of his survival needs

questions.question: Which detail from the text best supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: C. “The lake water had filled his stomach but left it hungry”
